claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know their age, and if you're claiming for multiple children, please tell me the ages of each child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello, yes I'm inquiring about my child Alex. He's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 13 years old, he falls into the **under 16** category, which is good news for eligibility purposes.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** in paid employment (or not working at all)?

This is typically not an issue for most 13-year-olds, but I need to confirm this as part of the assessment. 

- **Yes** - Alex works less than 24 hours per week or doesn't work
- **No** - Alex works 24 or more hours per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you! Since Alex doesn't work at all, he meets this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **NOT receiving any benefits in his own right**, such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, does Alex **not** claim these benefits himself?

- **Yes** - Alex does not receive these benefits in his own right
- **No** - Alex does receive ESA or Universal Credit in his own name

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex doesn't receive any benefits himself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently on an apprenticeship?

- **No** - Alex is not on any apprenticeship
- **Yes** - Alex is on an apprenticeship (please let me know which type and where in the UK)

For reference, most apprenticeships would disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except for Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't on any apprenticeship. He's just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, Alex is in regular school, so no apprenticeship issues there.

**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet **any one** of the following residency requirements?

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You are a resident of the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You live abroad as a Crown servant

Do any of these situations apply to you? (You only need to meet **one** of these to qualify)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Just to make sure I understand your situation correctly:

You mentioned you live abroad. Are you living abroad as a **Crown servant** (for example, working for the UK government overseas, such as in the diplomatic service, armed forces, or civil service posted abroad)?

Or are you living abroad in other circumstances?

This is important because Crown servants living abroad can still qualify for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad in other circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I've reached a conclusion about your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: NOT ELIGIBLE

I'm sorry to inform you that based on the information provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Reason:
You do not meet the **UK residency requirements** for Child Benefit.

### Summary for Alex:
While Alex himself meets all the child-related criteria (he's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not on an apprenticeship), the issue is that **you as the claimant** must normally live in the UK or meet specific alternative circumstances to claim Child Benefit.

### What would make you eligible?
To qualify, you would need to meet **one** of these conditions:
- Normally live in the UK
- Have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Have **pre-settled status** with qualifying criteria (working, seeking work, studying, or having sufficient resources)
- Be a **Crown servant** living abroad

### What you can do:
- If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you may then be able to claim Child Benefit
- If you believe you may qualify under any of the special circumstances I mentioned, you could contact HMRC directly to discuss your situation

For more information, you can visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I understand this may not be the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not on an apprenticeship - all of which would normally support eligibility. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They do not normally live in the UK, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and are not a Crown servant living abroad. UK residency (or qualifying circumstances) is a mandatory requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK, have settled/pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or be a Crown servant posted abroad.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not on an apprenticeship - all of which would normally support eligibility. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They do not normally live in the UK, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and are not a Crown servant living abroad. UK residency (or qualifying circumstances) is a mandatory requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK, have settled/pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or be a Crown servant posted abroad.
